retrieved_docs.title: Cat Eye Syndrome (Schmid-Fraccaro Syndrome) -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: At the genetic level, CES arises from a supernumerary marker chromosome, often dicentric, composed of material from chromosome 22. In approximately 90% of cases, this marker contains 2 extra copies of the proximal 22q11 region (tetrasomy), while a smaller proportion exhibits an additional copy (trisomy). The critical region encompasses approximately 1.5 to 2 Mb and includes multiple dosage-sensitive genes whose overexpression is believed to contribute to the diverse phenotypic features of CES. Molecular cytogenetic techniques, such as fluorescence in situ hybridization (FISH), array comparative genomic hybridization (aCGH), and, more recently, genome-wide single-nucleotide polymorphism (SNP) microarrays, have replaced traditional karyotyping for precise delineation of the supernumerary chromosome and identification of the breakpoints. This high-resolution genomic mapping is crucial for definitive diagnosis, genotype-phenotype correlations, and recurrence-risk counseling. [3]

retrieved_docs.title: DiGeorge Syndrome -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: A clinician makes a definitive diagnosis of DGS in individuals with a microdeletion of chromosome 22 at the 22q11.2 locus. Classic evaluations of genetic abnormalities, such as trisomies, including the Giemsa banding technique, are incapable of revealing microdeletions. Microdeletions responsible for DGS are therefore detected by fluorescence in situ hybridization (FISH), multiplex ligation-dependent probe amplification (MLPA), single nucleotide polymorphism (SNP) array, comparative genomic hybridization (CGH) microarray, or quantitative polymerase chain reaction (qPCR). The availability and cost of these techniques can delay diagnosis, particularly in resource-poor settings.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 12 -- Level 4 -- Community 2824
retrieved_docs.content: Genetics is recognized as an allowed qualifier for gout and other crystal arthropathies, for intestinal infection, and for فَرْطُ الدَّرَقِيَّةِ (hyperuricemia). This indicates that hereditary factors may influence susceptibility to these conditions, underscoring the relevance of genetic considerations in their diagnosis, risk assessment, and management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 4 -- Community 2885
retrieved_docs.content: Infant preterm low birth weight is clinically associated with short stature (قصر القامة), indicating that children born prematurely with low birth weight have an increased risk of developing reduced height later in life.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2900
retrieved_docs.content: CONDITIONS OF FETAL GROWTH AND MALNUTRITION represent a collective group of perinatal problems that encompass short gestation, low birth weight, and fetal growth retardation. Short gestation, defined as delivery before full term, often results in low birth weight because the infant has had insufficient time for normal growth. Low birth weight, in turn, frequently reflects fetal growth retardation, a condition in which the fetus does not achieve its genetically expected size. These three entities are closely interrelated, each contributing to the broader spectrum of fetal growth and malnutrition disorders, and together they increase the risk of adverse neonatal outcomes. Effective maternal care that optimizes nutrition and prevents premature delivery is essential for reducing the incidence of these interconnected conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 24 -- Level 1 -- Community 257
retrieved_docs.content: importance of assessing metastatic potential even when the primary lesion’s benign versus malignant nature is unclear. Collectively, these interrelated entities form a hierarchical and functional network that connects genetic syndromes, primary gastrointestinal cancers, various metastatic malignancies, and skin melanomas, providing essential context for accurate diagnosis, staging, and management in patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 3 -- Community 2605
retrieved_docs.content: INFANT PRETERM LOW BIRTH WEIGHT is clinically associated with قصر القامة (short stature), indicating that infants born preterm with low birth weight often develop reduced linear growth. In turn, قصر القامة is clinically associated with وفاة حديث الولادة (neonatal death), suggesting that short stature in this vulnerable population may be linked to an increased risk of death shortly after birth. The Pediatric Adverse Events Terminology encompasses adverse pediatric outcomes, and its subset includes the concepts تدلّي الحبل السري (umbilical cord prolapse) and وفاة حديث الولادة (neonatal death). Together, these relationships highlight a network of interrelated risk factors and adverse events: preterm low birth weight, short stature, and neonatal death are closely connected, while umbilical cord prolapse is also recognized within the same adverse‑event framework, underscoring the importance of comprehensive monitoring and intervention in high‑risk newborns.

Recognizing these interconnections is essential for accurate diagnosis, risk assessment, and safe medication selection in patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2239
retrieved_docs.content: Hemorrhage into nerve trunk, inflammatory and toxic neuropathy, and hereditary sensory neuropathy (اعتلال الأعصاب الحسي الوراثي) are all conditions in which the nerve serves as the primary site of pathological findings. This highlights that the nerve is a common locus for diverse neuropathic processes, ranging from acute hemorrhagic injury to inflammatory, toxic, and genetic sensory neuropathies. Recognizing the nerve as the affected structure helps clinicians focus diagnostic evaluation and tailor management strategies for patients presenting with these nerve‑related disorders.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 2 -- Community 1062
retrieved_docs.content: within nervous system disorders. Clinically, شقيقة and شقيقة فالجية عائلية are considered clinically similar, and شقيقة and صداع وعائي are described as related and possibly synonymous, suggesting substantial overlap in their clinical presentation and underlying mechanisms. Together, these relationships integrate migraine into a network of genetic, vascular, and systemic neurological conditions, underscore its diagnostic and therapeutic considerations, and illustrate its connections to both broader headache classifications and specific hereditary migraine phenotypes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 7 -- Level 1 -- Community 182

Overall, migraine (شقيقة) is interconnected with hereditary, vascular, and structural cerebral disorders, is classified across several hierarchical and synonymous relationships, and serves as both a specific clinical entity and a bridge to broader central nervous system disease concepts, which is essential for accurate diagnosis, genetic counseling, and comprehensive patient management.

This unified structure supports coding, diagnosis, and patient‑centered management of a broad array of neurological conditions, emphasizing the central role of peripheral nerves, pain, paralysis, and targeted surgical decompression in clinical practice.

Genetic and developmental contexts such as genitourinary congenital anomalies, sexually transmitted infections, childhood epilepsy, and Stickler syndrome fall under the congenital, familial, and genetic disorders SMQ, underscoring the overlap of hereditary factors with renal and systemic disease.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 15 -- Level 4 -- Community 2680
retrieved_docs.content: Short gestation, low birth weight, and fetal growth retardation are recognized as a related group of adverse perinatal outcomes and are classified together as a distinct clinical category.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 1449
retrieved_docs.content: Preterm low‑birth‑weight infants are clinically associated with قصر القامة (short stature). Short stature itself is clinically associated with وفاة حديث الولادة (neonatal death), indicating that children who remain short may have an increased risk of dying in the newborn period. Additionally, تهديد بالمخاض المبتسر (threatened preterm labor) is clinically associated with قصر القامة, suggesting that pregnancies complicated by early labor threats often result in offspring who develop short stature. Collectively, these relationships highlight short stature as a common clinical link among preterm low birth weight, threatened preterm labor, and adverse neonatal outcomes such as neonatal death, underscoring its relevance for risk assessment and early intervention in perinatal care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 25 -- Level 4 -- Community 2796
retrieved_docs.content: Allowed qualifier is identified as a genetic factor (genetika) associated with several specific conditions, including اِضطِراب اللسان (tongue disorder), فَرْطُ الدَّرَقِيَّةِ (excessive tearing), and فَرْطُ ضَغْطِ الدَّم العيني (increased intra‑ocular pressure). Recognizing this genetic linkage can aid clinicians in understanding the hereditary contributions to these disorders and may inform diagnostic evaluation and personalized management strategies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 2 -- Community 683
retrieved_docs.content: Overall, the network of relationships portrays diabetes as a multifaceted condition that influences and is influenced by genetic abnormalities, neonatal presentations, limb and ocular complications, metabolic comorbidities, diagnostic testing, and medication safety, all of which are essential considerations for comprehensive patient management.
